steps.title: Substitute into the function
steps.content: The function is $f(x)=4+\sqrt{x}$, so $f(144)$ means replace $x$ with $144$: $$f(144)=4+\sqrt{144}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate the square root
steps.content: The principal square root of $144$ is $12$. Therefore, $$f(144)=4+12=16.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: B is correct. The common trap is to mishandle the square root or evaluate the function at a different input; this question only asks for the value when $x=144$.
